channel.title: AI News Feed
channel.description: Curated AI news with Claude-powered summaries

item.title: Crimson Desert dev apologizes for use of AI art
item.description:
That’s some sloppy looking slop. | Image: Rex_Spy / Reddit
Reviews of Crimson Desert have been mixed, but the bigger issue for the game has been the discovery of what appeared to be AI-generated ass

item.title: Musk says he’s building Terafab chip plant in Austin, Texas
item.description:
Grok, how do you make chips? | Image: Laura Normand / The Verge
Elon Musk announced plans to build a Terafab plant in Austin, Texas, that will be jointly run by Tesla and SpaceX. The goal is to even

item.title: AI was everywhere at gaming’s big developer conference — except the games
item.description: AI was everywhere at the GDC Festival of Gaming this year. Vendors at the event pitched generative AI tools for things like making AI-driven NPCs and even entire games from a chat box. On the show flo

item.title: The gen AI Kool-Aid tastes like eugenics
item.description: Like many people, director Valerie Veatch was intrigued when OpenAI first released its Sora text-to-video generative AI model to the public in 2024. Though she didn't fully understand the technology,

item.title: Gemini task automation is slow, clunky, and super impressive
item.description:
An AI assistant that can actually get things done.
I've been testing out Gemini's new task automation on the Pixel 10 Pro and the Galaxy S26 Ultra, which for the first time lets Gemini take the whee

item.title: Trump takes another shot at dismantling state AI regulation
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday unveiled its new legislative blueprint for AI regulation, and the seven-point plan includes a clear message: The federal government should avoid many AI regulations

item.title: Google Search is now using AI to replace headlines
item.description: Since roughly the turn of the millennium, Google Search has been the bedrock of the web. People loved Google's trustworthy "10 blue links" search experience and its unspoken promise: The website you c

item.title: Amazon is making an Alexa phone
item.description: Over 10 years after shelving the Fire Phone, Amazon is reportedly planning to launch another smartphone, this time focused on Alexa. According to Reuters, the phone, which is code-named "Transformer,"

item.title: Why people really hate AI
item.description: There's a big, and increasing, disconnect in culture right now when it comes to artificial intelligence. Companies of all shapes and sizes are hunting for places to deploy AI and can't stop talking ab

item.title: OpenAI is planning a desktop ‘superapp’
item.description: OpenAI is working on a desktop "superapp" that merges its ChatGPT app, the Codex AI coding app, and its AI-powered Atlas browser into one app, The Wall Street Journal reports. The company is making th

item.title: The Download: OpenAI is building a fully automated researcher, and a psychedelic trial blind spot
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully automated

item.title: OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully automated researcher
item.description: OpenAI is refocusing its research efforts and throwing its resources into a new grand challenge. The San Francisco firm has set its sights on building what it calls an AI researcher, a fully automated

item.title: The Download: Quantum computing for health, and why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can sol

item.title: The Download: The Pentagon’s new AI plans, and next-gen nuclear reactors
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classifi

item.title: What do new nuclear reactors mean for waste?
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. The way the world cur

item.title: The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says
item.description: The Pentagon is discussing plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their models on classified data, MIT Technology Review has learned. A

item.title: The Download: OpenAI’s US military deal, and Grok’s CSAM lawsuit
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Where OpenAI’s technology could show up in Iran OpenAI has co

item.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B fundi

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powe

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team bui

item.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several la

item.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds
item.description: When the creator of the world's most advanced coding agent speaks, Silicon Valley doesn't just listen — it takes notes. For the past week, the engineering community has been dissecting a thread on X f

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite: Built for intelligence at scale
item.description: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is our fastest and most cost-efficient Gemini 3 series model yet.

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro: A smarter model for your most complex tasks
item.description: 3.1 Pro is designed for tasks where a simple answer isn’t enough.

item.title: A new way to express yourself: Gemini can now create music
item.description: The Gemini app now features our most advanced music generation model Lyria 3, empowering anyone to make 30-second tracks using text or images.

item.title: Accelerating discovery in India through AI-powered science and education
item.description: Google DeepMind brings National Partnerships for AI initiative to India, scaling AI for science and education

item.title: Gemini 3 Deep Think: Advancing science, research and engineering
item.description: Our most specialized reasoning mode is now updated to solve modern science, research and engineering challenges.

item.title: Accelerating Mathematical and Scientific Discovery with Gemini Deep Think
item.description: Research papers point to the growing impact of Deep Think across fields
